purpose: Current production bundle debt ceiling. The measured v3.51.22 CSV import client-integrity boundary produces 4,570,088 raw / 1,310,139 gzip JavaScript bytes. Against the committed v3.51.20 measured artifact (4,554,710 / 1,304,738) this is +15,378 raw / +5,401 gzip (under 0.42%) across the v3.51.21 server-error and v3.51.22 client-integrity boundaries: deterministic response validation, versioned guidance detection, source-index preservation, last-selection-wins parsing, duplicate-submit and mapping-collision guards, bounded error-body reads, accessibility semantics, and localized recovery copy. No database, Edge wire contract, CSS, dependency or speculative request timeout was added. The total ceilings retain only the existing 128-byte raw and 279-byte gzip cross-platform/SHA allowances; largest gzip retains the existing 35-byte allowance. Further increases require an explicit reviewed baseline update.
